Margaret Ebeling.review_text: BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD

Lectures feel very scripted since she mostly reads from notes and doesn't go into much depth on the material. It can come across as robotic and not very engaging. Projects also felt unnecessarily complicated for what we were learning. I'd consider other options if you can.
PLEASE WHATEVER YOU DO, DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS IF POSSIBLE! YOU WILL REGRET YOUR LIFE CHOICES

I would rather go to Thailand, learn Muay Thai, become a champion in Thailand, return to the U.S., join the UFC, go undefeated for 10 years, and then retire than to listen to another one of her lectures
Bad lecturer, reads from notes the whole time
This class is so boring and the professor just reads from notes. Not engaging at all. Do not take

Ebeling just directly reads off from pre-written notes, making for very boring lectures. Additionally, the grading for the projects never made much sense. While the content is stale and not very useful, just utilize flashcards to memorize all of the terms the day before the exam and it's easy to do well in the course.

Ebeling is a very caring professor and a great person. Unfortunately, the content and presentation of the material could use a major overhaul. Unengaging lectures and you can get a fairly good grade while taking no notes.

Make sure to really follow every part of the rubric for project submissions because they deduct for any little thing. She does the tests based directly off of the textbook and notetakers so make sure to take the time to read it and be in class. She likes making her class difficult and doesn't like to help.

She was nice but lectures were not engaging and hard to follow. She reads off a paper and slides. Deadlines are not easy to follow. Feedback is given on team projects but it doesn't match the criteria for the assignments. Would not take again.

pretty easy class in terms of content and work load, but don't expect to learn anything from the lectures. she will complain that people don't attend lectures, but all she does is waste time. she reads a script of fill in the blank notes rather than actually teaching. it's all about memorization and not at all understanding of marketing concepts.

She's kind, but the course is disorganized. Top Hat is unreliable, and losing points because of its errors isn't fair. If she knows the system is flawed, why use it? Assignments should be listed in the schedule, and grading needs improvement. Our graders didn't like the color of our slides/didn't know Hydro Flask was in the water bottle industry.

Ebeling is very chill. She makes the effort to relate to her students and make a connection with the course and our reality. She is kind of a boring lecturer but the note takers and study modules will prepare you to do well on the exams regardless. She is fun to talk to and pretty easy going.

I would rather watch paint dry then sit through another one of her lectures. The amount of time that she spends just reading from her little iPad is concerning. if you take this class bring AirPods and a pillow you WILL need them.

Class is a complete waste of time. She reads straight from her iPad the enter hour long class. Gives absolutely no feedback on group projects. But then again, not even surprised because she doesn't do any grading on her own, her grad TA's do all the grading. No clear grading rubric and docs off points for stupid reasons. Good luck...

Bring your pillow to class; Ebeling is boring. Every single word that comes out of her mouth is from an iPad she carries around the entire lecture. With that being said, Ebeling is a kind, passionate lady, which makes class slightly more tolerable.

If one eller board member watched her teach for 15 minutes she be fired on the spot. My only advice is after you receive your grade back on exam 1 and team project 1 you go and file a complaint with your concealer. This teacher is so incompetent she doesn't even know her rating is a 3.2.

Margaret is a sweet kind person but her teaching isn't for everyone. I really liked being around her and she has lots of passion but it is just not a fun class content wise. Make sure you show up for participation. tests were easy and you will get an A if you barely put in effort

Management and marketing are the biggest time suck and waste of time classes you will ever take. Don't forget to take quizzes and go to class. Group papers and projects are graded harshly for no reason. Expect to feel emptiness and despair after having to sit through Ebeling and Paradise drone on about nothing for 3 hours.

Class is so BORING! She reads off her iPad all class and is so monotone. I wouldn't go to class if I didn't have to but she does in class activities. I'd recommend taking notes on the textbook while in class because she reads from the textbook. There's 4 exams, but one is dropped. Projects are graded very heavily and feedback is confusing.

Not the most engaging lecturer in comparison to Paradise however quizzes and exams are much easier in this class than in management. Preceptors however are similarly useless; they'll lead you to believe your projects look good before being hit with a low B. Lots of points for attendance and lab research participation though. An A is obtainable.

Ebeling's lectures aren't the best. She reads off an iPad, and is very disengaging overall. Loves to take attendance, even on weeks like Thanksgiving or last day of instruction. The written group projects are graded unnecessarily difficult because they are graded by TA's who are trying to make the project seem harder than they really are.

I do not think Ms. Ebeling is a very good professor her lectures are not engaging and many of the assignments and exam questions almost seem ai generated. From what I understand she does almost zero grading seeing as how the TAs grade any group projects, and all exams and assignments are graded by the machine. Overall, there is room for improvement
I can't believe she had us rate her in class. If I were her, the last thing I would want would be for people to rate me. Terrible teacher, horrible communication and boring.

Ebeling is an extremely petty teacher who makes students feel like kindergarteners. Her lectures are her reading off her ipad and getting mad when students don't participate. Attendance is a huge part of your grade and you are expected to be in class. Gets mad at students for asking questions, overall just not a nice person.

Looking at past reviews she has definitely improved. There are 4 exams with your lowest grade dropped (unproctored taken online). The TA's do suck and the lectures are boring but she's a decent teacher. The only annoying this is attendance is mandatory and she loves to take it when she knows students will be gone.

This class is very straightforward, and very easy. Her lectures are somewhat dry but very predictable and easy to digest with the slides. If you read the syllabus, you can plan the entire semester out in advance, it's that predictable in terms of course load. If you have questions on the material, she's very enthusiastic about helping people learn.
Just show up to class and you'll be fine it's really not that bad.

Although her lectures can be boring, she is a very nice professor when it comes to one-on-one work. She is honest if an idea is not going to be graded well but offers alternate solutions that will be graded well. She wants to students to success, but she wants them to learn more than anything.
Not a hard course at ALL. You'll be fine
Very boring lecturer.

Professor doesn't have a clear attendance policy. Her syllabus states it is out of 25 points, but in D2L it is listed at 50. Every absence after 2 takes off 5 points out of the "25". She chose incompetent preceptors, and her lectures are so boring. She doesn't know how to teach. I'm sorry that you will have to take her class.

Yikes. She's the worst. Everyone failed every exam so horribly that she curved D's to B's and A's. Came from a non-teaching position straight to upper-division classes, and screwed us all over. Also! Attendance policy isn't addressed & doesn't take a dean's excuses. Every additional absence (past two) was 5 points taken from a possible 25 points.

Terrible teacher. She not only can't teach and just reads off the slides, but she is also insanely rude. There were 5 exams (6 with the take-home final), and they were all very difficult. Her study guides were excessively long and didn't even help. If you do all the extra credit and do well on the group project, you should be okay grade-wise.

The first exam was difficult because she pulled the questions from a question bank. The following exam questions were created by her. Everything you need to know will be on the study guide she created. Don't waste your time reading the textbook. The exams were insanely easy because I only followed her study guide. Got As on all the exams.

Extremely boring lectures that are read off of a script. You are better off not listening to the lectures and just reading each chapter, taking notes on your own. I got nothing less than a 79% on all 5 exams by doing this. It's a lot of work, but it will save your grade. Content isn't hard, just poor teaching :(

Richard Strickler.review_text: I have never has such a kind and caring professor - although he was not the most engaging lecturer, he was so approachable outside of the classroom, wanted us all to succeed, and gave so much extra credit after everyone failed the midterm.

Rick is a nice guy but I have no idea what he was talking about most the time in his lectures. He talks about random stories and things going on in the world rather than the actual material. Cheat sheet on the exam was clutch. Pretty easy A if you complete all the work. Take advantage of EC

He was not supposed to teach this class, but he tried his best to make it engaging and interesting. Vague when it came to assignment instructions and not the best lecturer, but doesnt grade harshly and offered 100+ pts in EC. Very caring, wanted everyone to get an A, and definitely didn't want to be a pain with everything else in 1st semester Eller

He was thrown into teaching 361 last minute and it shows. He's very very caring and nice but didn't do much to actually teach marketing concepts. Lectures were quite disengaging but the class was exceptionally easy.
He is such a sweet guy and cares so much about his students. The class was rocky at first, but he went in with an open mindset and helped out the students as much as possible.

He got thrown into teaching MKTG 361 at the last minute so first half of the semester was rough, but it's coming together now. Giving a 5 because every time I've come to ask a question he is so helpful, kind and supportive. Also  flexible and understanding with projects and homework. SO MUCH EXTRA CREDIT. Wants students to succeed.

One of the most genuinely happy professors. Always greeted us with a smile. Encouraged participation and offered so much extra credit. I enjoyed his lectures and the knowledge he shares as I felt that if you paid attention, it made so much sense. Started creating his own slides, and they were so cute with enriching videos.

This prof has no credibility and teaches nothing that related to graded material. He gets his slides directly from the textbook and when asked what material is on midterm, he responded I don't know and that it is randomly made up from the book. I have no idea how he has this job as professor in Eller. doesn't teach and doesn't care.

Lilian Almeida.review_text: Pretty easy class. Reading the textbook is better than her lectures and slides bc the material is explained better and has details that's not lectured but on exams. The group project is so easy and the grading is not tough. A little studying goes a long ways, and the exams are always way easier than quizzes given in class. Pretty straight forward.

Professor Almeida is an alright professor. You can tell she is somewhat new to teaching at the upper division however her class is fair to students. She is very strict however which can be annoying at times.

Almeida was not a bad professor, but I also never went to class after exam 2. Not her fault, was just hard to sit through another lecture until 5pm. If an attendance system was implemented, I am sure she would have more turnout during lectures. Her class is very straightforward, the textbook helps in understanding topics discussed in class.
Almeida made this class so fun! I really enjoyed attending her lectures. Her exams and quizzes are exactly what you see in her powerpoint slides, so study those and you'll do fine in her class.

Lilian is an amazing professor. She cares about all her students and it shows. Her lectures are engaging and fun. The tests only involve the material that she teaches in her slides so there are no surprises. I wish the U of A put her class before MGMT because hers is much more interesting and fun to be involved in.

She does not care. If you have a question during a test, forget getting any help from her. Multiple options on the test could technically be right but she has "her" right thought process. Her heavy "Extra Credit" assignments are worth 2 POINTS. Not useful AT ALL.

Victor Piscitello.review_text: Vic is a very passionate speaker and that reflects his style of teaching style. His style of teaching consists of him recycling old stories about his life that mildly relate to the course, rather than teaching the material on the tests. He also thinks hes the dopest thing since sliced bread.

This class is purely an outlet for Vic to gloat about himself, a huge ego stroke. His primary intention when grading is to find the most petty issues and mark of large portions of grade. He will call you out in the middle of class, just to humiliate you for not paying attention or even not knowing the answer to his question. He'll screw you over...

Vic is certainly funny and personable, but there is no way his class could be called easy, valuable, or enjoyable. Vic is like that uncle who has a lot of funny stories but you wouldn't want to leave alone with your girlfriend. As for content, all the exams are difficult and are taken straight from the book's test bank.

ELLER professor? I don't think he is qualified in this fantastic school what a shame professor. he calls on people infront of 300 people , DOES NOT cover any material in class and keep talking about random things. this cannot be an eller professor .. his language is so bad in class too. please eller fire him

Don't miss class until you're called on. Keep your head down if you don't want to be called on. Be prepared for false stats and pretty weird/inappropriate comments. Write down all the small stuff he says because he will quiz on it.  Study from the book for tests, not his lectures. Pay attention when he talks about the Phases. Good Luck!

Vic will never do what he says, everything is hard in this class there is no such help, exams r hard no study guide no curve, quizzes r popped, the other online homework is through an awful software. this class will break ur first semester at Eller if u take it with Vic

He's charming but this class is awful. Very, very difficult to get an A. Tests and pop quizzes include questions on really specific things from the book or guest speakers. You would think that Intro to Marketing shouldn't be a hard class but it was the hardest class I've taken so far in college
Terrible. Absolutely terrible. Lectures do not correlate to his pop quizzes or tests. Basically promotes cheating. Unfortunate that he is mandatory for every business student.

What can i say about this professor BAD BAD BAD, do not take his class unless u want to get a C or D, he does not respect students at all and does not help at all. all he talks about is his own life and own experiences so get ready for self teaching otherwise u will FAIL. He does not do any kind of help or give u extra credit. NEVER in this class.
HORRIBLE EXAMS, NO CURVES
Please take this advice into consideration, and don't take this class with Vic !!! thats all i can say
Lectures do not include anything that will be on your tests except maybe guest speakers. Stick to only reading the book. Small guy extremely big ego.
Very rude and does not respect students

Vic is one of the worst professors i ever had. Not professional, uses bad words in class and does not lecture but make fun of people and call on them. Quizzes are HARD like hell and he only lets u take it one time STAY AWAY FROM THIS PROFESSOR!!!!

His preceptors grade everything so he doesn't do much. Always goes on unnecessary tangents during lectures telling stories about his family or life. 5 pop quizzes on anything he went over in class or in the reading. Hard tests, 50 multiple choice. He randomly picks on students once a semester to answer a question.

Rough class and bad prof. Best advice is to bug the heck out of your preceptor and get good grades on your phases. Those count alot and if you don't follow exact directions, you will lose massive points. Best of luck!
terrible. rude and lectures are unrelated to tests and quizzes
Worst, most unfair  professor I have ever had the misfortune to take a class with. Make sure you catch every word he says in lecture or else you'll get a 20% on the pop quizzes.

This class is pretty straight forward.  Go to class, do the readings and assignments and you will get a good grade.  Vic is one of the laziest professors I have been around (does no work himself - doesn't grade, the powerpoints are from the publisher) so go to your TA if you have a question.  Lectures are entertaining but pretty worthless.
Don't bother going to class his lectures are useless, just read the book and you should be fine on exams.

GO TO CLASS. You do not want to miss his quizzes. The quizzes and exams are impossible and focus on the smallest details. The lectures themselves are entertaining, he is very passionate and enthusiastic but you will never learn anything relevant to the quizzes or exams.

The class itself is incredibly difficult. The "Introduction" part gets lost in having to know all the small and unimportant details from the book. Exams are near impossible to be able to prepare for. Go in to see your TA early on and always pay attention to Vic during class.

IF YOU ARE IN B COHORT NEVER SKIP. Yesterday he gave us a pop quiz(half a letter grade) two classes after the last exam we had barely covered any material and he did it just to punish the half the class that did not show and was a joke in comparison to his other quizzes (pretty much everyone got 80/100 quiz averages are like a 60 usually)

get ready to attend multiple lecturers that have nothing to do with exams or quizzes. This class by far has been the worst in my experience. I don't like to place blame on anyone, but the instructor had everything to do with my bad experience. Once you recieve your participation points don't attend any more lectures unless its quiz day.

Dude sucks. Literally, all he does during class is brag about how rich he is and how rich his family is. Asked him to fix something in the grade book for me, he said he cannot as he is on vacation. Bruh you get paid for this. None of the lecture material is on the test. Just a capper.

Try and get all the learnsmarts done before the semester starts. They are pure busy work and really interfere with more important things as time goes on. Lectures are fun but useless, make a point to go because Vic will take attendance for a grade or have a quiz if he sees a lot of people absent. Tests are rough just study everything in the book

Vic is one of the toughest instructors you will have in Eller, but boy is he worth it. You will not regret taking this course, if you listen! Pay attention, get to know him, because he really is an awesome guy. You won't find anyone more passionate about their job. Do your work and do it well!
He's a funny and entertaining guy. The projects can be vague and annoying though. Preceptors didn't help me at all and his tests really do suck. Overall not bad for first semester Eller.

Easily the WORST teacher I have ever had in my life. He spent all semester talking about his rich friends/family members. None of his exams are related to content he talks about in class. I know tons of 4.0 students who got their first B in his class. Intro to marketing is not supposed to be this difficult.
He is the worst teacher I've ever had.

One of the worst professors at UA. To call him a professor seems too much. His tests are highly unfair. He does not teach anything in class and going to class is absolutely useless. Even if you study, you wont do good on the test because he genuinely wants you to fail. Complete loser
This dude will not shut up. Talks about lots of random stuff. Super hard grader.
His humor gets old EXTREMELY fast

Basically you have to go to all of his lecture just to sit there and listen to him tell jokes, and try to convince the entire class that he is basically a billionaire. The class is almost completely self taught and for homework you have 1-2 80+ question learnsmarts due each week. The test should not be to bad if you dissect each chapter in depth.

Although Vic is a nice guy, his class sucks! Tests are beyond hard, and he will randomly call on you in front of everyone. He basically gets paid a boat load of money to tell stories (which can be interesting) and brag about how much money he has. Also, when my group needed some clarification for a paper, he was no help... see the preceptors.

Not much to say other than what has been said. Might be the worst teacher I have had at any level of school in my life. Truly disappointed this teacher works at UA. Such a shame. Good luck to anyone who has to take his class.

Vic is a funny guy, but his class is terrible. Be prepared for constant tangents about useless concepts. His participation system is broken but it still manages to force you to go to class. His tests are painful. This class really got under my skin

If you want to see a professor do absolutely do nothing(he doesn't grade the phases he assigns, doesn't write write his own tests, doesn't make his own PPs). This dude has the easiest job in the world. He comes, makes fun of students, acts like he writes the tests and is a marketing GOD. God this class really ruffled my feathers
Exams not written by himself. Team project grading not even close to fair.

He would yell at you in front of every one. He does a bad job at teaching. The worst professor ever. He doesn't teach any important concept. And the exams are very hard! All you can do is to buy the text book and study on your own. I would definitely avoid him if possible.

Sexual Innuendo 101 should be his class. He is very innappropriate, narcissistic, and condescending. You wish you could heckle him (without getting kicked out of class) because his attempts at humor are flat, canned, and lame. I can't believe he gets paid for having his TAs write such hard tests and being so raunchy and rude.

Vic is an enigma and I wouldn't be surprised if he is fired within the next few years. Playing the role of a salesman, Vic knows marketing well and truly sells it to his students.  In class, he is friendly and loves to joke.  But in office hours, beware, Vic will use inappropriate language, verbally attack you, and be very disrespectful.

The broad lectures are a waste of time and do not correlate with exams, which are very narrow and specific. Vic is also rude and gets mad at students for no reason. I emailed him asking to fix a mistake in D2L, and he yelled at me in the email with ALL CAPS! He is rude, uncaring, and unprofessional.

I managed to do well because I read the book and studied pretty much every article/video/guest lectures. Remember to read the syllabus a few times. Overall, I personally didnt think it was TOO difficult. He does, however, love to ask very random questions like how many vending machines are in japan. He also makes very creepy comments sometimes.
WORST PROFESSOR EVER. Never ever take him again for any other class once you're done with the first semester in Eller.

His tests are very weird. Same with quizzes. Hes obessessed with asking percentages on exams. His questions are very random. That being said, READ THE BOOK, watch the videos, read the articles, and you should be fine. If ur a good looking girl and the class is online, keep ur camera off bc if not he will 100% make u uncomfortable.

Easily the worst professor I have ever had. His lectures are useless as he often talks about stuff completely unrelated to marketing. He has two 70 question Smartbook assignments due every week. Includes obscure facts into his test that are almost impossible to know, making the tests even harder than they should be. Sometimes makes creepy comments.

This guy was the worst professor I have ever had in my entire school career, I have no idea why he is the professor for all 1st semester Eller. So rare to get an A and it's an INTRO class. His test questions are so unfair and random it's impossible to study, it's just random things because he genuinely wants to see you fail. Insanely unfair.

The worse professor Eller has to offer. minimum of 70 Qs per chapter, Quiz questions come deep from the videos he shows in class " not related to the content of class", he gives the worse exam study guides. exams are very hard. be ready to spend countless hours studying in the class with bad exam grades :)

Literally, no words. The answers on his test were literally incorrect from what he taught in class. His lectures are not helpful. His powerpoints contain like none of the content that is asked on exams. Just plain bad. Extremely disappointed that Eller has him as the core MKTG teacher for the first semester of upper classes.

He's not very nice at all in class and he is just boring. He spends most of the class not even talking about marketing but other random stuff thats either about his life or his favorite sports teams. One of if not the worst professors ever and most annoying in Eller. Do not take him at all for any class if you do good luck making through the class.

Lectures are him calling people out and asking them useless questions that do not help teach the content. He misleads students on what to focus on in exams, questions are unfairly specific (down to headings on certain slides) Better off just reading the textbook, class is a complete waste of time. Kicks people out of class for basically nothing.

I did not mind this guy. Most people do not care for him, but his lectures are really entertaining. He randomly picks people to ask questions in the lecture hall, so just sit there in bland clothing and avoid sitting under the bright lights in the room. His tests are a bit challenging, but the homework is a joke that you can do while watching tv.

Vic really likes to listen to the sound of his own voice and is very entertaining, however, it's hard to follow his lectures because he treats lectures as his story time. The homework does not relate to the tests at all and anything he says in class is fair game on the test, even if it doesn't relate to marketing. Good luck!

Tests are hard unless you have those test banks. His lectures & ppts arent good to learn, he just rants about his life. I found him obnoxious and disrespectful. NO PHONES or he freaks out. His grading is not clear or consistent. Worst teacher/person at eller. We reported him multiple times but hes the only option for MKTG so just push through :(

if at all possible, avoid Vic. 2-3 textbook assignments per week, all 80+ questions. Exams and quizzes are based on the smallest facts and figures from the lectures and textbooks. Pay close attention to what he says during class and take detailed notes, review slides before exams. Group project is easy way to make up lost points.

He really does a terrible job teaching anything. The textbook readings are not only really long but also can get tedious. Most of what he talks about in class too is not even related to marketing and it gets old after only a few weeks.

It's true, his quizzes are way too specific, but he is otherwise a great professor. His lectures are kind of random and seem to get off-topic, but if you listen to what he's talking about and what the topic is, you can see the connection. It definitely makes class much more interesting. His quizzes are tough, but I've heard he curves...

The reasons for why Vic is a horrible professor are endless. Class is useless, didn't learn a single thing because he loves to hear himself talk. Whats important to actually pass his exams/quizzes is the BOOK! Read the chapters carefully, study guides are useless. Definitions, statistics, percentages, the smallest things WILL be on his exam!

Did not enjoy this class at all. Vic goes on and on about the most insignificant things such as alcohol or sports and wastes our time. He tries to be funny, but after awhile, it gets old. He thinks he is hip and cool but in reality he is mean to his students and get angry really easily. Dont even bother studying for the quizzes bc they are random

Gave the fattest curve at the end of the semester. Nothing in the lectures is useful for the tests but at least he's entertaining. Just read the book chapters and do the learn smart homeworks and you'll be fine. A lot of extra credit too.

Absolutely terrible at teaching, going to class will actually make you lose brain cells because Vic doesnt value any of his students time. Vic rambles on about how much alcohol all of his students should be drinking and spends 50% of the class trying to get students engaged by asking them personal questions during the lecture. Horrible teacher:)

Vic is a professor that can take a bit of getting used to. The most important thing to do is read the book before classes so you can be prepared for his pop quizzes. They are difficult and if you get bad grades on them it will really screw your final grade. He has really helpful preceptors so make sure to utilize them for help on the Phases!

Vic spends the entire class talking about random things, being rude, and calling on people. You basically learn nothing in class but you have to go though because he calls random names and gives you points if you are there. There are also random quizzes. The tests aren't awful, just read the book. He also curves the class at the end of semester.

He's very loud and outgoing. Attendance is mandatory, especially the Monday before Thanksgiving. I never used the textbook, however his tests are hard without reading it. Use the online code and take practice tests or quizzes, some questions come straight from that. Go to preceptor or his office hours for help on papers.

Unclear, misleading tests.  Lectures are not related to course material. On an exam, I noticed two correct answers to one question.  I asked Vic and he agreed both were right.. What kind of test is that? He also specified only one answer would be correct.

Always come to class and ALWAYS read the book! It does way more in depth than he does. Sure, this is a difficult class, but it's NOT impossible to do well. If you read and take notes and study, you CAN do well! The lectures hold your attention.
Attend class - he gives hints about what will be on the next exam/quiz. Study the material as he goes over it, rather than waiting until the night before. Don't expect good grades on the exams.

People aren't lying when they say he is funny, but you don't laugh much when the average for an exam is a 60%.  He is very arrogant and rude, and his quizzes are just as hard as the exams. Not optional for most majors, but study like crazy! The lectures won't help you, and he calls you out in front of EVERYONE

Best Professor ever. Don't listen to these crybabies that whine about Vic, they're probably the up tight Accounting tights who wouldn't know a joke if it hit them in the face. Get a sense of humor, Vic's the BEST!!!!!!!!!
Enjoyable lectures, but not very helpful when asked questions outside of class.  Tests are difficult, make sure you read the book!
Great stand up comedian...horrible teacher.  Hit tests are hard, and his quizzes are ridiculous.  He does not teach anything, but he is very entertaining if you're looking for a show.

He just tells stories in class and does not teach anything.  Class is hard because the tests are so subjective and he does not cover any important material.  I would drop it if you have him and take it the following semester.

He likes to tell stories during lecture which can be amusing but don't relate to the tests.  Have to read the book for the tests.  Be prepared for some pretty random questions about percentages/names/numbers from assigned articles.  Also, he loves stereotyping and often makes **** remarks.
Vic makes a lot of irrelevant and inappropriate comments in class.  He is extremely personable, but it is hard to follow his lectures.  He is a nice guy, but the class is pretty difficult.
He is a very animated and interesting lecturer.  You will not mind going to class.  The tests, however, are difficult so make sure to use the book and online stuff as well as lecture notes to study.
Loved his class
Awesome guy!!  Classes are always educational and entertaining.  Vic has a wonderful personality!  Must have teacher for any business major!!
His class is fun, but the exam is a bit hard. Anyway, he tried his best!!!
Vic is the best professor at U of A. Hands down

I personally found Vic's lectures to be entertaining as he includes personal stories (some don't like his humor). He is a no BS kind of guy & will call you out on not paying attention/texting. Papers can be annoying; preceptors very helpful. Participation randomly called twice per semester. Quizzes tough; pay attention in lecture. Tests not so bad
Vic's kinda tough but if you show you're putting in the effort then he's willing to give some leeway.  If you slack off and act like you're uninterested, you're gunna get called out.
Vic's tests and quizzes are 100% from the book.  Lecture is honestly useless.  Funny guy though.  Read the book and study hard and the class is not that bad.

His tests are extremely difficult and completely irrelevant to what he lectures about in class. He loves talking about branding and diversity but there's much more to marketing than that. See his preceptors for help on doing well on project phases. This class will probably be your lowest grade first semester of Eller but there is a curve.
Loved his class! He puts a real world perspective on every concept he teaches to the class. Only class i actually could stay awake in!

I liked him, he's funny, very sarcastic. You will be called on, make sure you go to class or you will lose points. His tests are HARD, study slides, learnsmart and notes. It's not easy to get an A in this class. Pay attention to Phase 1-3 deliverable instructions, they aren't clear and you will lose points.

beat. 2/10 teacher. too hard for an INTRO to marketing class. Tests averages were 18-20% lower than the last semester for a reason. unfair is a word you will hear repeatedly. Have fun with this class as it is required for eller :)

Vic gets a bad rap. He's extremely funny and makes lectures super entertaining. Sometimes he says things that are a lil to savage but honestly anything to get me through an hour lecture. Class hard to get an A in because tests cannot be prepared for by studying. Pay attention in class and show up. Gives a decent amount of EC (White knight of biz)

Toughest A I've ever earned. Vic loves to ask "gotcha" questions on tests that pull from minutiae within the book. My study tip for his exams is to study the heck out of the chapters and remember *every* real-world example, case study, and percentage no matter how minor. Quizzes not as bad, study slides + any in-class guest speaker material. GL
Very good info throughout course, Vic focuses on getting students ready for the real world not just getting an A in the class

Take everything you know about college and throw it away. Vic is a tough instructor for sure, but if you actually take the time to go for help it's doable. You can't do first semester Eller alone, if you're struggling (and everyone does) go in for help.

all_reviews.review_text: BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD BAD

Lectures feel very scripted since she mostly reads from notes and doesn't go into much depth on the material. It can come across as robotic and not very engaging. Projects also felt unnecessarily complicated for what we were learning. I'd consider other options if you can.
PLEASE WHATEVER YOU DO, DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS IF POSSIBLE! YOU WILL REGRET YOUR LIFE CHOICES

I would rather go to Thailand, learn Muay Thai, become a champion in Thailand, return to the U.S., join the UFC, go undefeated for 10 years, and then retire than to listen to another one of her lectures
Bad lecturer, reads from notes the whole time
This class is so boring and the professor just reads from notes. Not engaging at all. Do not take

Ebeling just directly reads off from pre-written notes, making for very boring lectures. Additionally, the grading for the projects never made much sense. While the content is stale and not very useful, just utilize flashcards to memorize all of the terms the day before the exam and it's easy to do well in the course.

Ebeling is a very caring professor and a great person. Unfortunately, the content and presentation of the material could use a major overhaul. Unengaging lectures and you can get a fairly good grade while taking no notes.

Make sure to really follow every part of the rubric for project submissions because they deduct for any little thing. She does the tests based directly off of the textbook and notetakers so make sure to take the time to read it and be in class. She likes making her class difficult and doesn't like to help.

She was nice but lectures were not engaging and hard to follow. She reads off a paper and slides. Deadlines are not easy to follow. Feedback is given on team projects but it doesn't match the criteria for the assignments. Would not take again.

pretty easy class in terms of content and work load, but don't expect to learn anything from the lectures. she will complain that people don't attend lectures, but all she does is waste time. she reads a script of fill in the blank notes rather than actually teaching. it's all about memorization and not at all understanding of marketing concepts.

She's kind, but the course is disorganized. Top Hat is unreliable, and losing points because of its errors isn't fair. If she knows the system is flawed, why use it? Assignments should be listed in the schedule, and grading needs improvement. Our graders didn't like the color of our slides/didn't know Hydro Flask was in the water bottle industry.

Ebeling is very chill. She makes the effort to relate to her students and make a connection with the course and our reality. She is kind of a boring lecturer but the note takers and study modules will prepare you to do well on the exams regardless. She is fun to talk to and pretty easy going.

I would rather watch paint dry then sit through another one of her lectures. The amount of time that she spends just reading from her little iPad is concerning. if you take this class bring AirPods and a pillow you WILL need them.

Class is a complete waste of time. She reads straight from her iPad the enter hour long class. Gives absolutely no feedback on group projects. But then again, not even surprised because she doesn't do any grading on her own, her grad TA's do all the grading. No clear grading rubric and docs off points for stupid reasons. Good luck...

Bring your pillow to class; Ebeling is boring. Every single word that comes out of her mouth is from an iPad she carries around the entire lecture. With that being said, Ebeling is a kind, passionate lady, which makes class slightly more tolerable.

If one eller board member watched her teach for 15 minutes she be fired on the spot. My only advice is after you receive your grade back on exam 1 and team project 1 you go and file a complaint with your concealer. This teacher is so incompetent she doesn't even know her rating is a 3.2.

Margaret is a sweet kind person but her teaching isn't for everyone. I really liked being around her and she has lots of passion but it is just not a fun class content wise. Make sure you show up for participation. tests were easy and you will get an A if you barely put in effort

Management and marketing are the biggest time suck and waste of time classes you will ever take. Don't forget to take quizzes and go to class. Group papers and projects are graded harshly for no reason. Expect to feel emptiness and despair after having to sit through Ebeling and Paradise drone on about nothing for 3 hours.

Class is so BORING! She reads off her iPad all class and is so monotone. I wouldn't go to class if I didn't have to but she does in class activities. I'd recommend taking notes on the textbook while in class because she reads from the textbook. There's 4 exams, but one is dropped. Projects are graded very heavily and feedback is confusing.

Not the most engaging lecturer in comparison to Paradise however quizzes and exams are much easier in this class than in management. Preceptors however are similarly useless; they'll lead you to believe your projects look good before being hit with a low B. Lots of points for attendance and lab research participation though. An A is obtainable.

Ebeling's lectures aren't the best. She reads off an iPad, and is very disengaging overall. Loves to take attendance, even on weeks like Thanksgiving or last day of instruction. The written group projects are graded unnecessarily difficult because they are graded by TA's who are trying to make the project seem harder than they really are.

I do not think Ms. Ebeling is a very good professor her lectures are not engaging and many of the assignments and exam questions almost seem ai generated. From what I understand she does almost zero grading seeing as how the TAs grade any group projects, and all exams and assignments are graded by the machine. Overall, there is room for improvement
I can't believe she had us rate her in class. If I were her, the last thing I would want would be for people to rate me. Terrible teacher, horrible communication and boring.

Ebeling is an extremely petty teacher who makes students feel like kindergarteners. Her lectures are her reading off her ipad and getting mad when students don't participate. Attendance is a huge part of your grade and you are expected to be in class. Gets mad at students for asking questions, overall just not a nice person.

Looking at past reviews she has definitely improved. There are 4 exams with your lowest grade dropped (unproctored taken online). The TA's do suck and the lectures are boring but she's a decent teacher. The only annoying this is attendance is mandatory and she loves to take it when she knows students will be gone.

This class is very straightforward, and very easy. Her lectures are somewhat dry but very predictable and easy to digest with the slides. If you read the syllabus, you can plan the entire semester out in advance, it's that predictable in terms of course load. If you have questions on the material, she's very enthusiastic about helping people learn.
Just show up to class and you'll be fine it's really not that bad.

Although her lectures can be boring, she is a very nice professor when it comes to one-on-one work. She is honest if an idea is not going to be graded well but offers alternate solutions that will be graded well. She wants to students to success, but she wants them to learn more than anything.
Not a hard course at ALL. You'll be fine
Very boring lecturer.

Professor doesn't have a clear attendance policy. Her syllabus states it is out of 25 points, but in D2L it is listed at 50. Every absence after 2 takes off 5 points out of the "25". She chose incompetent preceptors, and her lectures are so boring. She doesn't know how to teach. I'm sorry that you will have to take her class.

Yikes. She's the worst. Everyone failed every exam so horribly that she curved D's to B's and A's. Came from a non-teaching position straight to upper-division classes, and screwed us all over. Also! Attendance policy isn't addressed & doesn't take a dean's excuses. Every additional absence (past two) was 5 points taken from a possible 25 points.

Terrible teacher. She not only can't teach and just reads off the slides, but she is also insanely rude. There were 5 exams (6 with the take-home final), and they were all very difficult. Her study guides were excessively long and didn't even help. If you do all the extra credit and do well on the group project, you should be okay grade-wise.

The first exam was difficult because she pulled the questions from a question bank. The following exam questions were created by her. Everything you need to know will be on the study guide she created. Don't waste your time reading the textbook. The exams were insanely easy because I only followed her study guide. Got As on all the exams.

Extremely boring lectures that are read off of a script. You are better off not listening to the lectures and just reading each chapter, taking notes on your own. I got nothing less than a 79% on all 5 exams by doing this. It's a lot of work, but it will save your grade. Content isn't hard, just poor teaching :(

I have never has such a kind and caring professor - although he was not the most engaging lecturer, he was so approachable outside of the classroom, wanted us all to succeed, and gave so much extra credit after everyone failed the midterm.

Rick is a nice guy but I have no idea what he was talking about most the time in his lectures. He talks about random stories and things going on in the world rather than the actual material. Cheat sheet on the exam was clutch. Pretty easy A if you complete all the work. Take advantage of EC

He was not supposed to teach this class, but he tried his best to make it engaging and interesting. Vague when it came to assignment instructions and not the best lecturer, but doesnt grade harshly and offered 100+ pts in EC. Very caring, wanted everyone to get an A, and definitely didn't want to be a pain with everything else in 1st semester Eller

He was thrown into teaching 361 last minute and it shows. He's very very caring and nice but didn't do much to actually teach marketing concepts. Lectures were quite disengaging but the class was exceptionally easy.
He is such a sweet guy and cares so much about his students. The class was rocky at first, but he went in with an open mindset and helped out the students as much as possible.

He got thrown into teaching MKTG 361 at the last minute so first half of the semester was rough, but it's coming together now. Giving a 5 because every time I've come to ask a question he is so helpful, kind and supportive. Also  flexible and understanding with projects and homework. SO MUCH EXTRA CREDIT. Wants students to succeed.

One of the most genuinely happy professors. Always greeted us with a smile. Encouraged participation and offered so much extra credit. I enjoyed his lectures and the knowledge he shares as I felt that if you paid attention, it made so much sense. Started creating his own slides, and they were so cute with enriching videos.
Awful

This prof has no credibility and teaches nothing that related to graded material. He gets his slides directly from the textbook and when asked what material is on midterm, he responded I don't know and that it is randomly made up from the book. I have no idea how he has this job as professor in Eller. doesn't teach and doesn't care.

Pretty easy class. Reading the textbook is better than her lectures and slides bc the material is explained better and has details that's not lectured but on exams. The group project is so easy and the grading is not tough. A little studying goes a long ways, and the exams are always way easier than quizzes given in class. Pretty straight forward.

Professor Almeida is an alright professor. You can tell she is somewhat new to teaching at the upper division however her class is fair to students. She is very strict however which can be annoying at times.

Almeida was not a bad professor, but I also never went to class after exam 2. Not her fault, was just hard to sit through another lecture until 5pm. If an attendance system was implemented, I am sure she would have more turnout during lectures. Her class is very straightforward, the textbook helps in understanding topics discussed in class.
Almeida made this class so fun! I really enjoyed attending her lectures. Her exams and quizzes are exactly what you see in her powerpoint slides, so study those and you'll do fine in her class.

Lilian is an amazing professor. She cares about all her students and it shows. Her lectures are engaging and fun. The tests only involve the material that she teaches in her slides so there are no surprises. I wish the U of A put her class before MGMT because hers is much more interesting and fun to be involved in.

She does not care. If you have a question during a test, forget getting any help from her. Multiple options on the test could technically be right but she has "her" right thought process. Her heavy "Extra Credit" assignments are worth 2 POINTS. Not useful AT ALL.

Vic is a very passionate speaker and that reflects his style of teaching style. His style of teaching consists of him recycling old stories about his life that mildly relate to the course, rather than teaching the material on the tests. He also thinks hes the dopest thing since sliced bread.

This class is purely an outlet for Vic to gloat about himself, a huge ego stroke. His primary intention when grading is to find the most petty issues and mark of large portions of grade. He will call you out in the middle of class, just to humiliate you for not paying attention or even not knowing the answer to his question. He'll screw you over...

Vic is certainly funny and personable, but there is no way his class could be called easy, valuable, or enjoyable. Vic is like that uncle who has a lot of funny stories but you wouldn't want to leave alone with your girlfriend. As for content, all the exams are difficult and are taken straight from the book's test bank.

ELLER professor? I don't think he is qualified in this fantastic school what a shame professor. he calls on people infront of 300 people , DOES NOT cover any material in class and keep talking about random things. this cannot be an eller professor .. his language is so bad in class too. please eller fire him

Don't miss class until you're called on. Keep your head down if you don't want to be called on. Be prepared for false stats and pretty weird/inappropriate comments. Write down all the small stuff he says because he will quiz on it.  Study from the book for tests, not his lectures. Pay attention when he talks about the Phases. Good Luck!

Vic will never do what he says, everything is hard in this class there is no such help, exams r hard no study guide no curve, quizzes r popped, the other online homework is through an awful software. this class will break ur first semester at Eller if u take it with Vic

He's charming but this class is awful. Very, very difficult to get an A. Tests and pop quizzes include questions on really specific things from the book or guest speakers. You would think that Intro to Marketing shouldn't be a hard class but it was the hardest class I've taken so far in college
Terrible. Absolutely terrible. Lectures do not correlate to his pop quizzes or tests. Basically promotes cheating. Unfortunate that he is mandatory for every business student.

What can i say about this professor BAD BAD BAD, do not take his class unless u want to get a C or D, he does not respect students at all and does not help at all. all he talks about is his own life and own experiences so get ready for self teaching otherwise u will FAIL. He does not do any kind of help or give u extra credit. NEVER in this class.
HORRIBLE EXAMS, NO CURVES
Please take this advice into consideration, and don't take this class with Vic !!! thats all i can say
Lectures do not include anything that will be on your tests except maybe guest speakers. Stick to only reading the book. Small guy extremely big ego.
Very rude and does not respect students

Vic is one of the worst professors i ever had. Not professional, uses bad words in class and does not lecture but make fun of people and call on them. Quizzes are HARD like hell and he only lets u take it one time STAY AWAY FROM THIS PROFESSOR!!!!

His preceptors grade everything so he doesn't do much. Always goes on unnecessary tangents during lectures telling stories about his family or life. 5 pop quizzes on anything he went over in class or in the reading. Hard tests, 50 multiple choice. He randomly picks on students once a semester to answer a question.

Rough class and bad prof. Best advice is to bug the heck out of your preceptor and get good grades on your phases. Those count alot and if you don't follow exact directions, you will lose massive points. Best of luck!
terrible. rude and lectures are unrelated to tests and quizzes
Worst, most unfair  professor I have ever had the misfortune to take a class with. Make sure you catch every word he says in lecture or else you'll get a 20% on the pop quizzes.

This class is pretty straight forward.  Go to class, do the readings and assignments and you will get a good grade.  Vic is one of the laziest professors I have been around (does no work himself - doesn't grade, the powerpoints are from the publisher) so go to your TA if you have a question.  Lectures are entertaining but pretty worthless.
Don't bother going to class his lectures are useless, just read the book and you should be fine on exams.

GO TO CLASS. You do not want to miss his quizzes. The quizzes and exams are impossible and focus on the smallest details. The lectures themselves are entertaining, he is very passionate and enthusiastic but you will never learn anything relevant to the quizzes or exams.

The class itself is incredibly difficult. The "Introduction" part gets lost in having to know all the small and unimportant details from the book. Exams are near impossible to be able to prepare for. Go in to see your TA early on and always pay attention to Vic during class.

IF YOU ARE IN B COHORT NEVER SKIP. Yesterday he gave us a pop quiz(half a letter grade) two classes after the last exam we had barely covered any material and he did it just to punish the half the class that did not show and was a joke in comparison to his other quizzes (pretty much everyone got 80/100 quiz averages are like a 60 usually)

get ready to attend multiple lecturers that have nothing to do with exams or quizzes. This class by far has been the worst in my experience. I don't like to place blame on anyone, but the instructor had everything to do with my bad experience. Once you recieve your participation points don't attend any more lectures unless its quiz day.

Dude sucks. Literally, all he does during class is brag about how rich he is and how rich his family is. Asked him to fix something in the grade book for me, he said he cannot as he is on vacation. Bruh you get paid for this. None of the lecture material is on the test. Just a capper.

Try and get all the learnsmarts done before the semester starts. They are pure busy work and really interfere with more important things as time goes on. Lectures are fun but useless, make a point to go because Vic will take attendance for a grade or have a quiz if he sees a lot of people absent. Tests are rough just study everything in the book

Vic is one of the toughest instructors you will have in Eller, but boy is he worth it. You will not regret taking this course, if you listen! Pay attention, get to know him, because he really is an awesome guy. You won't find anyone more passionate about their job. Do your work and do it well!
He's a funny and entertaining guy. The projects can be vague and annoying though. Preceptors didn't help me at all and his tests really do suck. Overall not bad for first semester Eller.

Easily the WORST teacher I have ever had in my life. He spent all semester talking about his rich friends/family members. None of his exams are related to content he talks about in class. I know tons of 4.0 students who got their first B in his class. Intro to marketing is not supposed to be this difficult.
He is the worst teacher I've ever had.

One of the worst professors at UA. To call him a professor seems too much. His tests are highly unfair. He does not teach anything in class and going to class is absolutely useless. Even if you study, you wont do good on the test because he genuinely wants you to fail. Complete loser
This dude will not shut up. Talks about lots of random stuff. Super hard grader.
His humor gets old EXTREMELY fast

Basically you have to go to all of his lecture just to sit there and listen to him tell jokes, and try to convince the entire class that he is basically a billionaire. The class is almost completely self taught and for homework you have 1-2 80+ question learnsmarts due each week. The test should not be to bad if you dissect each chapter in depth.

Although Vic is a nice guy, his class sucks! Tests are beyond hard, and he will randomly call on you in front of everyone. He basically gets paid a boat load of money to tell stories (which can be interesting) and brag about how much money he has. Also, when my group needed some clarification for a paper, he was no help... see the preceptors.

Not much to say other than what has been said. Might be the worst teacher I have had at any level of school in my life. Truly disappointed this teacher works at UA. Such a shame. Good luck to anyone who has to take his class.

Vic is a funny guy, but his class is terrible. Be prepared for constant tangents about useless concepts. His participation system is broken but it still manages to force you to go to class. His tests are painful. This class really got under my skin

If you want to see a professor do absolutely do nothing(he doesn't grade the phases he assigns, doesn't write write his own tests, doesn't make his own PPs). This dude has the easiest job in the world. He comes, makes fun of students, acts like he writes the tests and is a marketing GOD. God this class really ruffled my feathers
Exams not written by himself. Team project grading not even close to fair.

He would yell at you in front of every one. He does a bad job at teaching. The worst professor ever. He doesn't teach any important concept. And the exams are very hard! All you can do is to buy the text book and study on your own. I would definitely avoid him if possible.

Sexual Innuendo 101 should be his class. He is very innappropriate, narcissistic, and condescending. You wish you could heckle him (without getting kicked out of class) because his attempts at humor are flat, canned, and lame. I can't believe he gets paid for having his TAs write such hard tests and being so raunchy and rude.

Vic is an enigma and I wouldn't be surprised if he is fired within the next few years. Playing the role of a salesman, Vic knows marketing well and truly sells it to his students.  In class, he is friendly and loves to joke.  But in office hours, beware, Vic will use inappropriate language, verbally attack you, and be very disrespectful.

The broad lectures are a waste of time and do not correlate with exams, which are very narrow and specific. Vic is also rude and gets mad at students for no reason. I emailed him asking to fix a mistake in D2L, and he yelled at me in the email with ALL CAPS! He is rude, uncaring, and unprofessional.

I managed to do well because I read the book and studied pretty much every article/video/guest lectures. Remember to read the syllabus a few times. Overall, I personally didnt think it was TOO difficult. He does, however, love to ask very random questions like how many vending machines are in japan. He also makes very creepy comments sometimes.
WORST PROFESSOR EVER. Never ever take him again for any other class once you're done with the first semester in Eller.

His tests are very weird. Same with quizzes. Hes obessessed with asking percentages on exams. His questions are very random. That being said, READ THE BOOK, watch the videos, read the articles, and you should be fine. If ur a good looking girl and the class is online, keep ur camera off bc if not he will 100% make u uncomfortable.

Easily the worst professor I have ever had. His lectures are useless as he often talks about stuff completely unrelated to marketing. He has two 70 question Smartbook assignments due every week. Includes obscure facts into his test that are almost impossible to know, making the tests even harder than they should be. Sometimes makes creepy comments.

This guy was the worst professor I have ever had in my entire school career, I have no idea why he is the professor for all 1st semester Eller. So rare to get an A and it's an INTRO class. His test questions are so unfair and random it's impossible to study, it's just random things because he genuinely wants to see you fail. Insanely unfair.

The worse professor Eller has to offer. minimum of 70 Qs per chapter, Quiz questions come deep from the videos he shows in class " not related to the content of class", he gives the worse exam study guides. exams are very hard. be ready to spend countless hours studying in the class with bad exam grades :)

Literally, no words. The answers on his test were literally incorrect from what he taught in class. His lectures are not helpful. His powerpoints contain like none of the content that is asked on exams. Just plain bad. Extremely disappointed that Eller has him as the core MKTG teacher for the first semester of upper classes.

He's not very nice at all in class and he is just boring. He spends most of the class not even talking about marketing but other random stuff thats either about his life or his favorite sports teams. One of if not the worst professors ever and most annoying in Eller. Do not take him at all for any class if you do good luck making through the class.

Lectures are him calling people out and asking them useless questions that do not help teach the content. He misleads students on what to focus on in exams, questions are unfairly specific (down to headings on certain slides) Better off just reading the textbook, class is a complete waste of time. Kicks people out of class for basically nothing.

I did not mind this guy. Most people do not care for him, but his lectures are really entertaining. He randomly picks people to ask questions in the lecture hall, so just sit there in bland clothing and avoid sitting under the bright lights in the room. His tests are a bit challenging, but the homework is a joke that you can do while watching tv.

Vic really likes to listen to the sound of his own voice and is very entertaining, however, it's hard to follow his lectures because he treats lectures as his story time. The homework does not relate to the tests at all and anything he says in class is fair game on the test, even if it doesn't relate to marketing. Good luck!

Tests are hard unless you have those test banks. His lectures & ppts arent good to learn, he just rants about his life. I found him obnoxious and disrespectful. NO PHONES or he freaks out. His grading is not clear or consistent. Worst teacher/person at eller. We reported him multiple times but hes the only option for MKTG so just push through :(

if at all possible, avoid Vic. 2-3 textbook assignments per week, all 80+ questions. Exams and quizzes are based on the smallest facts and figures from the lectures and textbooks. Pay close attention to what he says during class and take detailed notes, review slides before exams. Group project is easy way to make up lost points.

He really does a terrible job teaching anything. The textbook readings are not only really long but also can get tedious. Most of what he talks about in class too is not even related to marketing and it gets old after only a few weeks.

It's true, his quizzes are way too specific, but he is otherwise a great professor. His lectures are kind of random and seem to get off-topic, but if you listen to what he's talking about and what the topic is, you can see the connection. It definitely makes class much more interesting. His quizzes are tough, but I've heard he curves...

The reasons for why Vic is a horrible professor are endless. Class is useless, didn't learn a single thing because he loves to hear himself talk. Whats important to actually pass his exams/quizzes is the BOOK! Read the chapters carefully, study guides are useless. Definitions, statistics, percentages, the smallest things WILL be on his exam!

Did not enjoy this class at all. Vic goes on and on about the most insignificant things such as alcohol or sports and wastes our time. He tries to be funny, but after awhile, it gets old. He thinks he is hip and cool but in reality he is mean to his students and get angry really easily. Dont even bother studying for the quizzes bc they are random

Gave the fattest curve at the end of the semester. Nothing in the lectures is useful for the tests but at least he's entertaining. Just read the book chapters and do the learn smart homeworks and you'll be fine. A lot of extra credit too.

Absolutely terrible at teaching, going to class will actually make you lose brain cells because Vic doesnt value any of his students time. Vic rambles on about how much alcohol all of his students should be drinking and spends 50% of the class trying to get students engaged by asking them personal questions during the lecture. Horrible teacher:)

Vic is a professor that can take a bit of getting used to. The most important thing to do is read the book before classes so you can be prepared for his pop quizzes. They are difficult and if you get bad grades on them it will really screw your final grade. He has really helpful preceptors so make sure to utilize them for help on the Phases!

Vic spends the entire class talking about random things, being rude, and calling on people. You basically learn nothing in class but you have to go though because he calls random names and gives you points if you are there. There are also random quizzes. The tests aren't awful, just read the book. He also curves the class at the end of semester.

He's very loud and outgoing. Attendance is mandatory, especially the Monday before Thanksgiving. I never used the textbook, however his tests are hard without reading it. Use the online code and take practice tests or quizzes, some questions come straight from that. Go to preceptor or his office hours for help on papers.

Unclear, misleading tests.  Lectures are not related to course material. On an exam, I noticed two correct answers to one question.  I asked Vic and he agreed both were right.. What kind of test is that? He also specified only one answer would be correct.

Always come to class and ALWAYS read the book! It does way more in depth than he does. Sure, this is a difficult class, but it's NOT impossible to do well. If you read and take notes and study, you CAN do well! The lectures hold your attention.
Attend class - he gives hints about what will be on the next exam/quiz. Study the material as he goes over it, rather than waiting until the night before. Don't expect good grades on the exams.

People aren't lying when they say he is funny, but you don't laugh much when the average for an exam is a 60%.  He is very arrogant and rude, and his quizzes are just as hard as the exams. Not optional for most majors, but study like crazy! The lectures won't help you, and he calls you out in front of EVERYONE

Best Professor ever. Don't listen to these crybabies that whine about Vic, they're probably the up tight Accounting tights who wouldn't know a joke if it hit them in the face. Get a sense of humor, Vic's the BEST!!!!!!!!!
Enjoyable lectures, but not very helpful when asked questions outside of class.  Tests are difficult, make sure you read the book!
Great stand up comedian...horrible teacher.  Hit tests are hard, and his quizzes are ridiculous.  He does not teach anything, but he is very entertaining if you're looking for a show.

He just tells stories in class and does not teach anything.  Class is hard because the tests are so subjective and he does not cover any important material.  I would drop it if you have him and take it the following semester.

He likes to tell stories during lecture which can be amusing but don't relate to the tests.  Have to read the book for the tests.  Be prepared for some pretty random questions about percentages/names/numbers from assigned articles.  Also, he loves stereotyping and often makes **** remarks.
Vic makes a lot of irrelevant and inappropriate comments in class.  He is extremely personable, but it is hard to follow his lectures.  He is a nice guy, but the class is pretty difficult.
He is a very animated and interesting lecturer.  You will not mind going to class.  The tests, however, are difficult so make sure to use the book and online stuff as well as lecture notes to study.
Loved his class
Awesome guy!!  Classes are always educational and entertaining.  Vic has a wonderful personality!  Must have teacher for any business major!!
His class is fun, but the exam is a bit hard. Anyway, he tried his best!!!
Vic is the best professor at U of A. Hands down

I personally found Vic's lectures to be entertaining as he includes personal stories (some don't like his humor). He is a no BS kind of guy & will call you out on not paying attention/texting. Papers can be annoying; preceptors very helpful. Participation randomly called twice per semester. Quizzes tough; pay attention in lecture. Tests not so bad
Vic's kinda tough but if you show you're putting in the effort then he's willing to give some leeway.  If you slack off and act like you're uninterested, you're gunna get called out.
Vic's tests and quizzes are 100% from the book.  Lecture is honestly useless.  Funny guy though.  Read the book and study hard and the class is not that bad.

His tests are extremely difficult and completely irrelevant to what he lectures about in class. He loves talking about branding and diversity but there's much more to marketing than that. See his preceptors for help on doing well on project phases. This class will probably be your lowest grade first semester of Eller but there is a curve.
Loved his class! He puts a real world perspective on every concept he teaches to the class. Only class i actually could stay awake in!

I liked him, he's funny, very sarcastic. You will be called on, make sure you go to class or you will lose points. His tests are HARD, study slides, learnsmart and notes. It's not easy to get an A in this class. Pay attention to Phase 1-3 deliverable instructions, they aren't clear and you will lose points.

beat. 2/10 teacher. too hard for an INTRO to marketing class. Tests averages were 18-20% lower than the last semester for a reason. unfair is a word you will hear repeatedly. Have fun with this class as it is required for eller :)

Vic gets a bad rap. He's extremely funny and makes lectures super entertaining. Sometimes he says things that are a lil to savage but honestly anything to get me through an hour lecture. Class hard to get an A in because tests cannot be prepared for by studying. Pay attention in class and show up. Gives a decent amount of EC (White knight of biz)

Toughest A I've ever earned. Vic loves to ask "gotcha" questions on tests that pull from minutiae within the book. My study tip for his exams is to study the heck out of the chapters and remember *every* real-world example, case study, and percentage no matter how minor. Quizzes not as bad, study slides + any in-class guest speaker material. GL
Very good info throughout course, Vic focuses on getting students ready for the real world not just getting an A in the class

Take everything you know about college and throw it away. Vic is a tough instructor for sure, but if you actually take the time to go for help it's doable. You can't do first semester Eller alone, if you're struggling (and everyone does) go in for help.
